form_analysis.form_header_text: FY27 Appropriations Programmatic and Policy Requests -- Rep. Melanie Stansbury (NM-01)
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Please complete the form below to requests funding and/or submit legislative or report language requests. Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration (AG); Commerce, Justice, and Science, Financial Services and General Government (CJS); Legislative Branch; Military Construction and Veterans Affairs (MILCON VA); and National Security and Department of State (formerly State-Foreign Operations/SFOPS), Defense (HACD) appropriations requests are due March 6th. Energy and Water Development (E&W); Homeland Security; Interior and Environment (Interior); Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education (Labor-HHS); Transportation and Housing and Urban Development (THUD) appropriations requests are due March 11th. Please contact StansburySubmissions@mail.house.gov with any questions or additional supporting documents with the subject line "ORGANIZATION NAME_Appropriations". Please note that this form is NOT for Community Project Funding requests. Additional information will be released as soon as we receive guidance from the House Appropriations Committee. NOTE: The form does not automatically save your responses. We suggest drafting your submission in a separate document to avoid losing any progress

sections.description: Information about the requesting organization and its representatives.

fields.label: Requesting Organization
Primary Point of Contact Name
Primary Point of Contact Email
Primary Point of Contact Phone
Secondary Point of Contact Name
Secondary Point of Contact Email
Secondary Point of Contact Phone
What is your affiliation with New Mexico or New Mexico's First Congressional District?

sections.description: General details about the type and category of your request.

fields.label: If your�organization is submitting for more than one request, please provide a ranking for this request (i.e. first, second, third priority)
fields.rationale: The ranking of this specific request relative to other organization-wide submissions is unknown and requires the submitter's internal priority assessment.

fields.label: Which Appropriations Subcommittee is this request for?
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs
Transportation, Housing, and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA funding falls under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: What agency/department is this request under?�
fields.rationale: Directly identifies the agency for the Science Mission Directorate as per reference values.

fields.label: What account is this request under?�
fields.rationale: Uses the specific NASA account name provided in the reference values.

fields.label: If you have been working with a staffer, please write their name here.
fields.rationale: This requires specific personal knowledge of the user's interactions with Congressional staff.

sections.description: Specific information regarding the narrative and funding for the request.

fields.label: Request Description
fields.draft_value:
* $9 billion for NASA Science Mission Directorate, a $1.75 billion increase over FY2026 enacted levels.
* Strategic investment to maintain U.S. leadership in space exploration and counter rapid advancements by international competitors.
* Full support for National Academies' Decadal Survey priorities, including Mars Sample Return and lunar exploration.
* Vital resources for maintaining a specialized aerospace and innovation workforce across the United States.
fields.rationale: Directly uses the $9B funding ask, the calculated net increase over FY2026, and standard advocacy points regarding leadership and competition.

fields.label: What kind of request are you submitting?
fields.rationale: The request is for a $9 billion funding level for the NASA Science Mission Directorate, which is a programmatic funding request.

fields.label: What was the enacted funding level in FY25? (Put "N/A" if for a report/bill language, or no funding was mentioned)
fields.help_text: Please provide the numeric dollar amount for FY25.
fields.rationale: Based on the SMD Topline Funding History provided in reference values for FY2025 Enacted. The pre-filled value of $7.25B appears to be for FY26.

fields.label: What was the enacted funding level in FY26? (Put "N/A" if for a report/bill language, or no funding was mentioned)
fields.help_text: Please provide the numeric dollar amount for FY26.
fields.rationale: Matches the FY2026 Enacted amount and pre-filled annotation provided in the reference values.

fields.label: What is the FY27 President's Request?��(Put "N/A" if not sure)
fields.rationale: Reference values indicate the FY2027 President's Budget Request has not been released yet.

fields.label: What is your FY27 Requested Funding Level?
fields.rationale: This is the specific funding ask for NASA's Science Mission Directorate provided in the reference values.

sections.description: Details for legislative or report language and letter-based requests.

fields.label: Has this language been included in previous bills or reports? If yes, please identify where and when it was included
fields.draft_value: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is an existing program consistently funded in prior CJS appropriations bills, most recently in H.R. 6938 for FY2026.
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request for an existing program as noted in the reference values and funding history.

fields.label: If requesting report or bill language, please share exact language or funding request here.
fields.draft_value:
* Programmatic Funding Request: $9,000,000,000
* Account: Science Mission Directorate
* Agency: NASA
fields.rationale: Summarizes the programmatic funding request as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Is your request a multimember letter or a request to have Rep. Stansbury lead a letter?
options: Request to have Rep. Stansbury lead a letter
fields.rationale: The reference context indicates that a multi-member letter exists (led by Reps. Bacon and Chu) which the submitter would be asking the member to join.

fields.label: If your request is for a multi-member letter, who are the leads?
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA)
fields.rationale: These individuals are identified as the leads for the existing House letter in the reference values.

fields.label: If your request is for a multi-member letter,�what is the letter title and/or link?�
fields.draft_value: Bipartisan House letter in support of NASA Science Mission Directorate funding.
fields.rationale: The reference values confirm an existing letter led by Reps. Bacon and Chu exists.

sections.description: Historical context and community impact justification.

fields.label: Did you make this request with our office last fiscal year?
fields.rationale: Whether this specific organization submitted this request to Rep. Stansbury's office in the previous fiscal year is not provided in the context and is known only to the submitter.

fields.label: If you are making this request to other Congressional Offices, please share them here.�
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request. Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: Please explain how this request will benefit communities in New Mexico and the First Congressional District.
fields.draft_value:
* Over $6.3M in recent NASA Science obligations supporting Albuquerque research and high-tech small business manufacturing.
* University of New Mexico research ($9.7M in total obligations across recent fiscal years) for lunar spacecraft technology, exoplanet discovery, and wildfire modeling.
* Direct economic impact for NM-01 contractors including Loadpath, LLC ($315k) and Westwind Computer Products ($333k).
* Sustained investment in the local STEM pipeline, inspiring the next generation of New Mexico's aerospace talent.
fields.rationale: Cites specific spending data for NM-01 including obligations total, UNM grant values, and specific contract recipients (Loadpath, Westwind).

fields.label: Please include any additional details or information you would like our staff to know
fields.draft_value:
* 3:1 economic return on every dollar invested in NASA Science, driving local innovation and high-tech demand.
* Bipartisan consensus on protecting NASA's mission from proposed budget instability, as seen in the FY 2026 restoration.
* Nonpartisan, peer-reviewed Decadal Survey roadmaps ensuring maximum scientific breakthrough for federal investment.
* Critical frontline in the new space race, setting international norms for 21st-century exploration and discovery.
fields.rationale: Draws on strategic advocacy talking points including economic ROI, bipartisan support context, and international competition.

form_analysis.deadline_text: March 6th (AG, CJS, Leg Branch, MILCON VA, National Security, Defense); March 11th (Energy and Water, Homeland Security, Interior, Labor-HHS, THUD)

top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Providing necessary water and sewer infrastructure support services.

top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing small spacecraft technology for Moon missions.
Discovering nearby, cooler exoplanets using the TESS satellite.

top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress.   Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond.   Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines   develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Supporting lunar, Martian, and applied science exploration initiatives.

top_grants.description: 23-fsit23-0019  contributing expertise in applied pre- and post-fire vegetation modeling to the firesense implementation team
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Assessing wildfire impact through advanced vegetation modeling expertise.
